h1: How to Prepare for Your Fireplace Installation
h2: Congrats on the New Fireplace! Here’s What To Do First
How to Get the Modern Farmhouse Living Room Look
10 Reasons to Add a Gas Fireplace to Your Home

paragraphs: Having a fireplace installed in your home is cause for celebration, but there are some essential preparations you must make before flipping the switch on your new hearth. While your local authorized dealer will walk you through the process, here’s a quick run-down of what to expect.
Leading up to installation, and afterwards, there will be several appointments (and possibly inspections) to ensure that your hearth is installed correctly and safely.
To prepare for a smooth installation, make sure to do the following before the technician’s arrival:
Congratulations—your hearth is up and running! Keep these helpful hints in mind after installation:

Follow these easy steps—and the lead of your authorized installer—and you’ll be basking in the glow of your brand-new fireplace in no time. Ready to get started on your project? Request a no-obligation consultation today.
Run your new fireplace through a burn-off cycle before you start using it regularly.
See how one interior designer makes her client’s modern farmhouse dreams come true, putting fireplaces center stage in living rooms of every size.
Warmth isn’t the only reason adding a gas fireplace is a sound investment.
Browse our photo gallery to find a fireplace design that reflects your unique style.
